comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Audit setup progress: searched for an existing architecture/OSS audit and created this task when none fit. Recorded read-only file impact. First plan-review attempt exposed the missing `[agents]` extra; remediated with the documented local `.venv` path and verified the pre-commit hook. Split the compound acceptance criterion per deterministic P6 coaching. Second review reached the live provider but returned INDETERMINATE because ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (and alternative provider credentials) are unset in this session. Per the mandatory gate protocol, the ticket remains open and unclaimed; no production-code audit has started. Next step: operator supplies a supported model credential in the execution environment, then re-run `rebar review-plan 1d3c-547d-f0cf-426c` until signed PASS and `rebar claim 1d3c-547d-f0cf-426c --assignee Codex`.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Audit retry on 2026-07-19: mandatory review-plan fetched shared state and reached the Anthropic provider, but individual model passes repeatedly hit transport retries and the workflow produced no verdict after an extended bounded wait. The process was interrupted; no plan-review attestation was issued, the ticket remains open/unclaimed, and no repository quality-tool inspection was performed. Next step: restore reliable provider connectivity and rerun review-plan, or explicitly authorize the audited claim --force path.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Operator explicitly authorized force after the live plan-review workflow repeatedly stalled on provider transport retries without issuing an attestation.".

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Automated quality-enforcement audit slice completed 2026-07-19.

Existing strengths:
- Gerrit Verified reuses the same lint, type-check, pip-audit, and test workflow as branch CI; the matrix covers Python 3.11-3.13 and macOS.
- Ruff is exactly pinned and gates E/F/W/I/UP/B plus BLE001, T201, and SIM115; mypy gates all 354 source files with a strictness ratchet.
- Branch coverage has a 70 percent floor; module size has a locked 800-LOC hard cap; generated schema, prompt, env-var, CLI, MCP, docs-index, and config drift gates are blocking.
- The LLM-Review lane makes gitleaks and the curated Semgrep-compatible High/Critical rules fail closed on changed files. CodeQL scans Python and JavaScript on main and weekly.
- Clean-wheel, optional-extra, README golden-path, actionlint, zizmor release audit, and dependency-vulnerability checks materially strengthen packaging and CI reliability.

Prioritized findings:
HIGH 1. CodeQL is post-merge only: codeql.yml triggers on main push and weekly schedule, not on the exact Gerrit patchset, and uses only default queries. Add an exact-patchset pre-merge CodeQL lane, security-extended queries, and an explicit SARIF severity policy; keep the weekly full scan. CONTRIBUTING currently claims mirror PR coverage that the workflow does not declare.
HIGH 2. The pre-merge scanner host is not reproducible: Dockerfile.reviewbot resolves the latest gitleaks release dynamically and installs unconstrained semgrep; the base image is tag-only. Pin scanner versions, hashes, Python requirements, and image digests. Many non-release Actions also use mutable major tags; expand zizmor from release.yml to every workflow and SHA-pin third-party actions.
HIGH 3. Dependency auditing is not reproducible or complete across extras: CI resolves unconstrained dependency floors on every run and pip-audit covers the dev closure, while reviewbot, ui, eval, tracing, and grounding extras are not all audited as shipped combinations. Commit a CI/dev lock or hashed constraints, install frozen, and audit core plus a supported union of production extras.
MEDIUM 4. The job named import-linter is an AST optional-dependency guard, not an architectural dependency-direction gate. Add Import Linter or equivalent contracts for stable layer boundaries and cycles, based on docs/architecture.md.
MEDIUM 5. The metric detector declares max_complexity 15, but engine_b_backends implements only file LOC and explicitly leaves function complexity as follow-up. Add Ruff C901 or a pinned lizard/complexity gate with a no-new-violations baseline; keep the 800-LOC cap.
MEDIUM 6. Mypy remains permissive outside rebar.graph and rebar.grounding: ignore_missing_imports is global and only those packages disallow untyped definitions. Continue the existing shrink-only ratchet and add high-signal global flags such as warn_unused_ignores, warn_redundant_casts, no_implicit_optional, strict_equality, and disallow_any_generics after baselining.
MEDIUM 7. Coverage has seven points of intentional headroom and omits subprocess-driven CLI coverage; mutation testing is configured but absent from CI and mutmut is not a declared dev dependency. Enable coverage subprocess patching, add changed-line coverage, raise the floor near the measured baseline, and run bounded scheduled mutation tests with per-module minimum scores.
MEDIUM 8. Ruff can add high-signal families incrementally rather than enable ALL: S, ASYNC, DTZ, RUF, and a calibrated complexity rule. Expand the tiny curated Semgrep ruleset first in advisory mode, then promote proven rules.
MEDIUM 9. Local hooks are not hermetic: pre-commit uses language system and Make calls ambient ruff and mypy. In this audit, ambient make lint used a different Ruff behavior on the ignored generated build-info file and ambient make typecheck could not find mypy, while direct .venv binaries passed. Route Make and hooks through the repository environment or a frozen runner.

Verification:
- Direct .venv Ruff check and format-check passed.
- Direct .venv mypy passed over 354 source files.
- Nine focused ratchet/actionlint/module-size tests passed.
- Security-rule freshness passed at 19 days against a 90-day cadence.
- Local semgrep could not initialize its X509 trust store; gitleaks 8.30.1 was present; lizard and scc were absent. These local facts do not substitute for the separately built production review-bot image.

No production files were modified.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Follow-up assessment for approved recommendations 5, 6, and 9: measured remediation required before enabling gates.

Complexity C901:
- Threshold 10: 154 current failures.
- Threshold 15: 52 failures.
- Threshold 20: 24 failures.
- Threshold 25: 13 failures.
- Threshold 30: 9 failures; maximum observed complexity is 44.
Recommendation: do not use per-file ignores because they allow new complex functions in exempt files, and do not add 52 inline noqas. Add a checked-in symbol-level baseline keyed by path, function, and current score. Gate all unlisted functions at 15 and fail if any baselined score increases or a new symbol appears; baseline entries may only shrink. Prioritize the nine functions above 30 for refactoring, then drain 25, 20, and 15 bands.

Mypy:
- no_implicit_optional: zero failures, enable immediately.
- strict_equality: zero failures, enable immediately.
- warn_redundant_casts: one failure, remove the cast in llm/workflow/editor.py, then enable.
- warn_unused_ignores: 23 failures, mostly stale reconciler ignores plus two workflow/schema ignores; remove or revalidate them, then enable.
- disallow_any_generics: 831 failures, so global enablement is not viable. Even the existing strict graph and grounding packages have only nine failures; fix those nine and add disallow_any_generics to their existing override. Continue package-by-package.
- disallow_untyped_defs: 585 whole-tree failures, but reducer, store, snapshot, schemas, review_bot, and engine_support total only ten failures. Annotate those ten definitions and promote those packages into the existing shrink-only strict override before tackling llm, commands, and engine.

Ruff additions:
- ASYNC has zero production findings and can be enabled for src now. Tests have one ASYNC110; fix or narrowly exempt that test.
- DTZ has three production findings; fix timezone handling in validate_checks and security_pin, then enable.
- RUF has 87 RUF100 stale-noqa findings under the full rule set, 55 mechanical findings, and 76 ambiguous-Unicode findings. Remove the 87 stale noqas after review; auto-fix/safely review the 55 mechanical cases; defer RUF001/2/3 with explicit code-level ignores because many symbols are intentional UI/prose. Then enable the selected RUF subset, not the whole family.
- S has 160 subprocess findings, 54 silent-exception findings, 32 production asserts, 20 URL-open findings, and a smaller 35-item high-risk set including tar extraction, XML parsing, MD5, public binds, chmod, temp paths, and timezone/randomness checks. Triage and remediate the 35 high-risk findings first. Enable proven individual S codes. Do not globally enable S603/S607 until subprocess execution is centralized behind the existing process seam or each call is validated; do not blanket-ignore whole files. Review S110/S112 for logging or justified inline suppression. Convert runtime asserts to explicit errors or justify invariant-only cases.
- Tests should permanently ignore S101 and likely S603/S607 at test scope; otherwise over 2,000 test assertions plus about 900 subprocess findings swamp the signal.

Semgrep:
- The local binary cannot validate or scan even with metrics and version checks disabled because its X509 trust store initializes with no anchors. Recommendation 2 must first pin the container scanner and add a build-time offline validate plus known-positive/known-negative fixture scan.
- Add broader vendored rules in advisory, diff-scoped mode first. Capture a main-branch SARIF baseline and promote rules individually only after false-positive review. No production cleanup is required before advisory rollout; blocking promotion requires a clean or explicitly adjudicated rule baseline.

No files were modified.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
Perform a read-only principal-engineer audit of rebar against modern software architecture and open-source community standards. Examine README.md, pyproject.toml, src/rebar/, tests/, docs/architecture.md, docs/adr/, CI workflows, packaging metadata, security posture, extension contracts, and contributor governance.

## Plan
- Map subsystem boundaries, dependency direction, data ownership, public APIs, CLI/MCP adapters, and the git-backed event store.
- Inspect tests, CI, release/packaging, security, documentation, accessibility of contribution, and project governance.
- Ground material findings in exact repository evidence and distinguish strengths, risks, and tradeoffs.
- Produce a prioritized remediation roadmap without modifying production code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The audit explains the architecture and identifies evidence-backed strengths.
- [ ] Each material risk is assigned a clear severity.
- [ ] Each material risk includes concrete code or documentation citations.
- [ ] The assessment covers OSS baseline artifacts, packaging, CI, security, governance, contributor experience, testing, and operability.
- [ ] Recommendations distinguish near-term fixes from longer-term architectural investments.
- [ ] Audit observations and final conclusions are recorded on this ticket.

file_impact.reason: Read-only review of project entry point and contributor positioning

Read-only review of architectural boundaries and implementation

Read-only review of CI, release, security, and OSS governance

compiled_state.title: Principal architecture and OSS standards audit
